channel.title: Bender, Adam on Chege's Blog
channel.description: Recent content in Bender, Adam on Chege's Blog

item.title: Testing in a Monorepo
item.description:
Testing Web Components

While any test framework can work, it’s better to test web components in a browser environment because that’s where they’ll be used. Node-based frameworks would require too much shimming of DOM calls that’d make the tests unrepresentative. and are good options for browser-based testing.

is powered by ES-build, and so is the client-side of the app; let’s go down this path and see where it leads. CommonJS modules don’t work in the browser . Earlier on , using ESM on the server turned out difficult. Hopefully, that doesn’t me bite back now.

item.title: Testing Your Code
item.description:
Why Write Tests

Helps catch bugs in the code, and in the programmer’s mental model of what the code does.

The later a bug is caught in the development cycle, the more expensive it is to fix it. A good test today is a future debugging session saved.

The test is the first client of your code. It uncovers sub-optimal design choices, tight couplings, missed cases, etc..

Some test failures are hard to debug, and slow down development, e.g. flaky tests.
